info.title: Amazon Lambda Tools for .NET Core applications (3.2.3
info.description: Amazon.Lambda.Tools adds commands to the dotnet cli to deploy AWS Lambda functions.

commands.name: delete-function
commands.description: Command to delete a Lambda function

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --project-location
options.description: The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: --persist-config-file
options.description: If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: --function-name
options.description: Lambda function name

commands.name: deploy-function
commands.description: Command to deploy the project to AWS Lambda

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --project-location
options.description: The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: --persist-config-file
options.description: If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: --configuration
options.description: Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: --framework
options.description: Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp2.1.

options.name: --msbuild-parameters
options.description: Additional msbuild parameters passed to the 'dotnet publish' command. Add quotes around the value if the value contains spaces.

options.name: --package
options.description: Application package to use for deployment, skips building the project

options.name: --function-name
options.description: Lambda function name

options.name: --function-description
options.description: Lambda function description

options.name: --function-publish
options.description: Publish a new version as an atomic operation

options.name: --function-handler
options.description: Handler for the function <assembly>::<type>::<method>

options.name: --function-memory-size
options.description: The amount of memory, in MB, your Lambda function is given

options.name: --function-role
options.description: The IAM role that Lambda assumes when it executes your function

options.name: --function-timeout
options.description: The function execution timeout in seconds

options.name: --function-runtime
options.description: The runtime environment for the Lambda function

options.name: --function-layers
options.description: Comma delimited list of Lambda layer version arns

options.name: --tags
options.description: AWS tags to apply. Format is <name1>=<value1>;<name2>=<value2>

options.name: --function-subnets
options.description: Comma delimited list of subnet ids if your function references resources in a VPC

options.name: --function-security-groups
options.description: Comma delimited list of security group ids if your function references resources in a VPC

options.name: --dead-letter-target-arn
options.description: Target ARN of an SNS topic or SQS Queue for the Dead Letter Queue

options.name: --tracing-mode
options.description: Configures when AWS X-Ray should trace the function. Valid values: PassThrough or Active

options.name: --environment-variables
options.description: Environment variables set for the function. For existing functions this replaces the current environment variables. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: --append-environment-variables
options.description: Append environment variables to the existing set of environment variables for the function. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: --kms-key
options.description: Key ARN of a customer key used to encrypt the function's environment variables

options.name: --apply-defaults
options.description: Obsolete: as of version 3.0.0.0 defaults are always applied.

options.name: --s3-bucket
options.description: bucket to upload the build output

options.name: --s3-prefix
options.description: prefix for for the build output

options.name: --disable-version-check
options.description: Disable the .NET Core version check. Only for advanced usage.
